explanation: No mutation applied. The command line is already a valid test case matching the Sigma rule.
Single quotes are used instead of double quotes to enclose the path. This is a safe mutation technique (substitution) and is still parsed correctly by the command interpreter.
Quotes are omitted around the path. This is a safe mutation technique (omission) and is still valid if the path does not contain spaces or special characters.
An extra space is added at the end of the command line. This is a safe mutation technique (insertion) and does not affect the parsing or execution.
The flag '-Path' is changed to lowercase '-path'. This is a safe mutation technique (substitution) and is still valid if the program accepts case-insensitive flags.
